high.title: CrateError `kind` discriminant is used at 6 throw-sites but never defined; client-side error categories absent from the §7 class hierarchy
high.where: SDD §3 (line 93), §3.1 (101), §3.2 (109), §3.4 (123), §4 (132), §6 (150) all throw CrateError{kind:'validation'|'not_found'|'parse'|'pagination'} — but §7 (156-164) defines only an abstract CrateError root + CrateAPIError/CrateNetworkError/CrateTimeoutError/CrateAbortError, with no `kind` field on any class and no Validation/Parse/Pagination/NotFound class or guard. The contradiction is inherited verbatim from design-verify-synthesis.md (kind used at lines 8/15/22/58/65/75/87, but the error-model decision at line 29 omits it). VERIFIED: real, load-bearing.
high.fix: Reconcile §7 with the throw-sites. Pick one: (a) add a discriminated `kind: 'api'|'network'|'timeout'|'abort'|'validation'|'parse'|'pagination'|'not_found'` field on base CrateError, make it concretely constructable for the four client-side kinds, and add isCrateValidationError/isCrateParseError/isCratePaginationError/isCrateNotFoundError brand guards; or (b) add concrete subclasses CrateValidationError/CrateParseError/CratePaginationError/CrateNotFoundError (literal this.name, brand inheritance, matching guards) and replace every `CrateError{kind:'…'}` reference with the class. Make §3/§3.1/§3.2/§3.4/§4/§6 and §7 agree, and add the new guards to the §7 brand-guard list.

high.title: search() params is untyped — the SearchParams interface the prior pass made a MUST-FIX was dropped entirely
high.where: SDD §3 surface map (line 77: `search(params?, opts?) → SearchResponse`, params UNTYPED), §2 type list (line 37, return types only), §4 (line 128, serialization mechanics only). The design-verify-synthesis explicitly DECIDED this (lines 8, 12, 69) and ranked the omission a [medium] must-fix (lines 88-89): type faceted params string|string[] per spec anyOf + re-export coverage. No SearchParams appears anywhere in the SDD. VERIFIED against spec: GET /api/v1/search has 19 query params; EIGHT are anyOf string|array — genre, style, format, country, label, cube_quadrant, exclude_artist, exclude_label (synthesis named only 3; spec has 8).
high.fix: Add a SearchParams interface to §2/§3 from the spec's /search parameters: the eight faceted params (genre/style/format/country/label/cube_quadrant/exclude_artist/exclude_label) typed string|string[]; the five *_mode siblings typed 'and'|'or'; q, year_from/year_to, dj_count_min, limit, offset per their spec scalar types. State array facets serialize by repeat-key (the §4 note already anticipates this) and pin it in the §4 query-serialization test. Apply the same to bandcamp.bulk params (source/cursor/limit). Correct the count to 8 faceted params, not 3.

medium.title: Four retry-tuning keys are referenced in §5 with user-facing affordances but have no field in the §3 options interfaces
medium.fix: §5 (lines 141-142) names maxBackoffMs, maxRetryAfterMs, totalDeadlineMs ('finite default 120000, null to opt out'), perAttemptTimeoutMs (='timeout') — but §3 CrateOptions/RequestOptions (lines 51-62) expose only timeout/maxRetries/headers. Decide and state: if user-tunable, add maxBackoffMs?, maxRetryAfterMs?, totalDeadlineMs?: number|null to CrateOptions (and RequestOptions if per-call-overridable) with defaults; if internal constants, say so and drop the 'null to opt out' affordance. Clarify perAttemptTimeoutMs is the internal name for the public `timeout` (single source of truth).

medium.title: Public request-body types are not re-exported, and ArtistDossierContract is listed twice in the §2 alias enumeration
medium.fix: §2 (line 37) claims aliases for 'every public-method return type' but the beacon methods (§3.4: ObservedBeaconRequest/RefinedBeaconRequest) and wayfind methods take a REQUEST body as the primary param, and none are exported — a consumer cannot name the object they must construct. All four (ObservedBeaconRequest, RefinedBeaconRequest, WayfindAnswerRequest, WayfindInterpretRequest) plus SearchParams and RateLimited exist verbatim in components.schemas (verified). De-dupe the duplicate ArtistDossierContract in line 37; add the request types + SearchParams to the alias set; broaden the §2 sentence to 'every public-method request AND return type'. Keep the §3.4 note that the SDK injects the REQUIRED `timestamp` (verified required on both beacon schemas) when omitted.

medium.title: MIT-on-a-commercial-API mitigation is locked but the shipped-artifact disclosure that makes it defensible is unspecified
medium.fix: jani LOCKED LICENSE=MIT (do not relitigate), but the synthesis flagged MIT-on-a-Commercial-ToS API as a [critical] misrepresentation (design-verify-synthesis.md:72-73) whose mitigation is a visible code-vs-access distinction. As written the rationale lives only in §8/§13 (internal grimoire); nothing in the published tarball (files:[dist,README,LICENSE]) tells a consumer MIT covers the code, not API access. Add a shippable disclosure requirement: (1) a README notice 'MIT applies to this client library's source; access to the crate API is governed separately by crate's Terms of Service and requires a valid API key; MIT grants no right to use the crate service'; (2) the same one-line notice prepended to LICENSE or a NOTICE file added to files:[]. Note in §8 this disclosure is the load-bearing half of the locked MIT decision.

medium.title: masters(ids) client-side validation documents only the ≤100 cap; spec also requires minItems:1 and positive bounded-integer ids
medium.fix: Spec masters/batch body (verified): ids minItems:1, maxItems:100, items integer exclusiveMinimum:0 maximum:2000000000. §3 (line 86) only states '≤100 ids'. The SDD already adopts fail-fast client-side CrateError{kind:'validation'} for key-gated and beacon-missing-token cases; an empty array sails to a server 400 the rest of the surface pre-empts. Extend the masters() guard to throw on empty (length<1) or >100. Document that the per-id integer-range bound (1..2_000_000_000) is server-enforced (returns 400) so the one-sided cap isn't mistaken for full validation. Same for master(id)/dossier.master(id): note id is a positive integer ≤2_000_000_000 (server-delegated).

integration_notes:
Apply before sprint-planning (none are blockers; SDD is implementable but has two HIGH spec-incompleteness defects an engineer must resolve):

1. (HIGH, §7 + §3/§3.1/§3.2/§3.4/§4/§6) Define the CrateError.kind discriminant OR add concrete client-side subclasses (validation/parse/pagination/not_found) + brand guards, and make every throw-site agree with §7. This contradiction was inherited verbatim from the synthesis (which also uses `kind` without defining it).

2. (HIGH, §2/§3/§4) Add the SearchParams interface (and bandcamp.bulk params type). EIGHT facets are anyOf string|array per spec (genre/style/format/country/label/cube_quadrant/exclude_artist/exclude_label) — the synthesis under-named them as 3; fix the count. Pin array repeat-key serialization in the §4 test. Add the existing §2 coverage-test ('fails if any public-method return schema lacks an alias') a request-type counterpart.

3. (MEDIUM, §3/§5) Resolve the retry-config-key surface: either expose maxBackoffMs/maxRetryAfterMs/totalDeadlineMs in CrateOptions or declare them internal constants and drop the 'null to opt out' wording.

4. (MEDIUM, §2/§3.4) De-dupe ArtistDossierContract in the line-37 alias list; add ObservedBeaconRequest, RefinedBeaconRequest, WayfindAnswerRequest, WayfindInterpretRequest, SearchParams, and RateLimited to the re-exports; broaden the §2 rule to 'request AND return types'.

5. (MEDIUM, §8) Add a shippable MIT-vs-API-access disclosure (README notice + NOTICE/LICENSE preamble). The MIT choice is locked, but the disclosure is the unmitigated half of the synthesis's [critical] license finding.

6. (MEDIUM, §3) Extend masters(ids) client-side validation to minItems:1 (not just ≤100); note per-id integer bound and master-id positivity are server-enforced.

Cosmetic (fold in cheaply): §8 misquotes info.license as bare string 'Commercial' — spec is an object {name:'Commercial — see Terms of Service'}; quote info.license.name. Add a one-line §14 note recording the anon-auth basis (no per-op security AND no global security — both verified absent in spec) + a contract test asserting no global security object appears, so an upstream auth-tier shift trips CI. Add a §5 one-liner that Retry-After/X-RateLimit-*/requestId headers are spec-undeclared (verified: zero response headers anywhere in spec; RateLimited body retry_after_seconds is the only contract-guaranteed delay floor — required:true verified) so the header-first precedence reads as intentional, not an assumption. Note in §6 that BandcampRow resolves to Record<string,unknown> (spec rows.items = additionalProperties:{}, verified) and adjust its contract-test expectation accordingly.

NOT a defect / dropped from the consolidated list:
- 'PRD scope-override never traced' (HIGH as filed): downgrade to a §13/§14 paperwork note, not a blocker. The full-surface expansion is in the task's LOCKED set ('V1 = FULL public surface incl. key-gated + beacon (jani)'). Still worth adding the dated supersede note to §13/§14 ('expanded from PRD FR1–FR9 to full surface per jani 2026-06-26, superseding PRD §4/§6') and either backfilling FR10–FR13 or adding §14 rows for the 6 PRD-excluded endpoints, but it is a traceability hygiene fix, not unimplementability.
- '§2 path-indexing rationale is a non-sequitur' (LOW): verified — all 20 typed 2xx responses are pure $refs to components.schemas (only 204s and /openapi.json are inline, neither typed). The path-indexing backbone adds helper-type plumbing with no payoff over direct components['schemas'] indexing. Optional simplification; correct the rationale if kept.
- Live-smoke matrix-amplification (LOW): one optional sentence ('runs on a single runner, opt-in, SHOULD route through the SDK retry path'); not material.
